title: Port forwarding large number of ports fails as the port is already in use
body:
### Issue Description

Trying to forward 1000 ports on an nginx container fails as podman assigns a port which is already in use.

```
podman run -d -p 80/tcp -p 443/tcp -p 500/tcp -p 501/tcp -p 502/tcp ............ -p 999/tcp  -p 200/udp -p 201/udp ...... -p 699/udp -it <nginx_base_image> 


Failed to bind port 41283 (Address already in use) for option '-u 41283-41283:539-539'

```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.Full command
```
podman run -d -p 80/tcp -p 443/tcp -p 500/tcp -p 501/tcp -p 502/tcp -p 503/tcp -p 504/tcp -p 505/tcp -p 506/tcp -p 507/tcp -p 508/tcp -p 509/tcp -p 510/tcp -p 511/tcp -p 512/tcp -p 513/tcp -p 514/tcp 2. Need to stop the container and run this command multiple times if the issue is not seen
```


### Describe the results you received

Run container fails as the port was already in use and podman assigned a used port to the container port.

### Describe the results you expected

Container should be running without any issues.

comments.body:
I ran this in a type loop 100 times and was not able to trigger it. I do notice I have a slightly newer pasta however.

```
baude@fedora podman (main) $ rpm -qf /usr/bin/pasta
passt-0^20240906.g6b38f07-1.fc40.x86_64
baude@fedora podman (main) $ pasta --version
pasta 0^20240906.g6b38f07-1.fc40.x86_64
```

This is basically a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10205 (just different symptom to trigger it), see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10205#issuecomment-1010055023
The main issue is that we assign random ports on create time and then when the processes actually binds it might already be used. As pointed out there fixing this is non trivial as it must work with all our different port forwarders which is a lot of work.
